questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The virus, originally found in animals, became a serious concern once it showed ______ transmission, making it a dangerous ______ threat.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Zoonotic, Pandemic. 💢 Detailed Explanation: • The word zoonotic refers to a disease that can be transmitted from animals to humans. In the sentence, it correctly describes the kind of transmission the virus initially exhibited. • The word pandemic describes a disease that spreads across countries or the entire world, affecting a large population. It fits the idea of a "dangerous threat" that goes beyond local or regional levels. Let’s see Other Options: • Widespread – व्यापक रूप से फैला हुआ– Happening in many places or among many people. Example: “There was widespread panic after the announcement.” • Contagious – संक्रामक / फैलनेवाला – Able to be transmitted from one person to another. • Example: “The flu is highly contagious in crowded places.” • Indigenous – स्वदेशी / मूलनिवासी – Native to a particular region or environment. • Example: “These herbs are indigenous to the Amazon rainforest.” • Local – स्थानीय – Related to or restricted to a specific area or region. • Example: “The local outbreak was brought under control within a week.” • Endemic– स्थानिक – Regularly found in a particular area or among a specific group of people. • Example: “Cholera was once endemic in many parts of India.” • Native– जन्मजात / देशज – Originating in a particular place or environment. • Example: “The tiger is native to several parts of Asia.”

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: The manager said that the team
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. should discussed the strategy Sol: Replace “discussed” with “discuss.” 📌 Concept – Modal verbs such as “should” must always be followed by the base form [first form] of the verb - not a conjugated form like “discussed.” 👉 So, the correct sentence should be: “The manager said that the team should discuss the strategy before the presentation begins.” ✅ 📌 👉 💢

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence.
options: No sooner did the results declared than the candidates started celebrating their success.
The manager as well as his assistants were responsible for completing the report on time.
She is one of the employees who has completed the compliance training successfully.
The officer asked the applicant whether he had submitted all the required documents before the deadline.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. The officer asked the applicant whether he had submitted all the required documents before the deadline. 💢 Option (1) - This sentence violates the rule of correlative conjunctions. • The correct structure is “No sooner did + v1… than …” • Here, the verb “declared” should be in base form → “declare.” 💢 Option (2) - This sentence violates subject–verb agreement. • The real subject is “manager.” • The phrase “as well as his assistants” does not affect the verb. • The verb should be “was responsible”. 💢 Option (3) - This sentence violates the rule of relative clause agreement. • The clause “who has completed” refers to “employees,” not “one.” • Therefore, the verb should be “have completed.” 💢 Option (4) - This sentence follows all grammatical rules correctly. • The reporting verb “asked” is in the past tense. • The earlier action is correctly expressed using past perfect (“had submitted”). • Tense sequence, narration, and sentence structure are correct. • Hence, this is the grammatically correct sentence.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Despatch. Sol: The correct spelling is - Despatch. • Catach - Catch - पकड़ना – To seize or gasp something,especially something moving. • Despatch - प्रेषण - to send something or someone quickly • Effecient - Efficient - कुशल – achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort or expenses. • Lappad - Lapped - घिरा हुआ – overlapped or wrapped around something

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no correction is needed, select ‘No correction required’. He insisted that she will complete the assigned task before the end of the day.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. would complete. 📌 Concept:- After verbs like insist, suggest, recommend, demand, the verb in the subordinate clause must follow reported speech tense consistency. 👉 Since “insisted” is in the past tense, “will” changes to “would” in indirect speech.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence Rohit Mehra belongs to an affluent family.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Loaded. 👉 Let’s know the meaning of all words - • Impoverished - निर्धन - Reduced to a state of poverty or financial hardship • Example: The family was impoverished after the loss of their business. • Indigent - दरिद्र - Suffering from extreme poverty or need • Example: The charity helps to support indigent families in the community. • Loaded - धनी - Having a lot of money, wealth, or resources • Example: The loaded businessman invested in several successful ventures. • Accord - समझौता - A formal agreement or harmony between parties • Example: The two nations signed an accord to promote peace and trade.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Fortify
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Undermine. 👉 Let’s know the meaning of all words - • Festal - उत्सवी - Related to a festival or celebration Example: The festal atmosphere was electric with music and dance. • Liberal - उदार - Open-minded or generous Example: She had a liberal approach to helping those in need. • Latent - सुप्त - Hidden or not yet developed Example: The latent talent in the child was discovered by the teacher. • Undermine - कमजोर करना - To weaken or damage something Example: The criticism aimed to undermine the leader's authority.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the following sentence. At the team retreat, Melissa, the stoic, sat quietly in a corner, jotting down her thoughts, while Kevin, the altruist, led ice-breaking games and shared funny stories to lighten the mood.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. introvert, extrovert . 💢 Detailed Explanation: • Melissa’s quiet, reflective behaviour suggests she is an introvert, not necessarily stoic [which implies emotional endurance, not reserved personality]. • Kevin’s social, lively behaviour fits the description of an extrovert more than an altruist [his actions are social rather than self-sacrificing]. 👉 Let’s see the meaning of all the key words used in the options and the question, along with their usage: • Stoic – भावनाहीन / सहनशीलव्यक्ति – Someone who can endure pain or hardship without showing their feelings or complaining. • Example: “The stoic accepted the bad news calmly and without emotion.” • Altruist – परोपकारी / निस्वार्थव्यक्ति – A person who has a genuine concern for the welfare of others and acts selflessly. • Example: “The altruist donated most of his earnings to charity.” • Introvert – अंतर्मुखीव्यक्ति – A person who tends to be more focused on internal thoughts and feelings rather than external stimulation or social interaction. • Example: “The introvert preferred reading a book over attending a party.” • Extrovert – बहिर्मुखीव्यक्ति – A sociable and outgoing person who enjoys interacting with others. • Example: “As an extrovert, he thrived in large gatherings and lively discussions.” • Realist – यथार्थवादीव्यक्ति – A person who accepts situations as they are and deals with them in a practical manner. • Example: “As a realist, she knew not everything would go according to plan.” • Idealist – आदर्शवादीव्यक्ति – A person who is guided more by ideals or values than by practical considerations. • Example: “The idealist dreamed of a world without poverty or war.” • Cynic – निंदक / अविश्वासीव्यक्ति – A person who believes that people are motivated purely by self-interest rather than acting for honourable reasons. • Example: “The cynic doubted the politician’s promises, calling them empty words.” So, option A [introvert, extrovert] is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Rule to roost
options: To exercise complete control or authority over a place or group
To follow rules strictly without questioning them
To establish new regulations in an organization
To take responsibility for someone else’s actions
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. To exercise complete control or authority over a place or group. 💢 Explanation: The idiom “rule the roost” means to dominate, control, or be the most powerful person in a place or situation. 👉 A “roost” is a place where birds (especially chickens) rest, and the one that “rules” it is the dominant one. Hence, the meaning aligns with exercising authority or control. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: The idiom comes from farm life, where the strongest or most aggressive bird controls the roosting place. Over time, it came to describe people who dominate others in a group, family, or organization. Example: In the office, the senior manager clearly rules the roost and makes all major decisions. Hindi: “rule the roost” = हावी होना / प्रभुत्व जमाना / सब पर नियंत्रण रखना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: win the race in the long run.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. win the race in the long run. Sol: Replace “win” with “wins.” 💢 The phrase “slow and steady” is treated as a singular concept [a proverb-like idea], so it should take a singular verb — “wins,” not “win.” 👉 So, the correct sentence is: “The coach reminded the athletes that slow and steady wins the race in the long run.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Catastrophe
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Debacle. 👉 Let’s explain meaning of all words - • Catastrophe -विपत्ति - A disaster or calamity. • Example - The earthquake was a catastrophe for the small town. • Debacle - बड़ी असफलता - A complete failure or disaster. • Example - The company's financial crisis ended in a debacle. • Madden - क्रोधित करना - To make someone extremely angry or annoyed. • Example - The delay in the project madden the client. • Taboo - वर्जित - A social or cultural prohibition. • Example - Discussing death is considered a taboo in many cultures. • Salvation - मुक्ति - The act of saving someone or something from harm or danger. • Example - The rescue team worked tirelessly for the salvation of the trapped miners.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Sita was rather disgruntled at the way she had been treated by the servant.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Contented. • Disgruntled - असंतुष्ट - Unhappy or dissatisfied . 👉 Let’s see meaning of all words - • Dissatisfied - असंतुष्ट - Unhappy or displeased - He was dissatisfied with the service. • Aggrieved - पीड़ित - Unhappy or offended - The aggrieved customer demanded a refund. • Annoyed - परेशान - Irritated or bothered - She was annoyed by the loud noise. • Contented - संतुष्ट - Satisfied or happy - She felt contented after receiving the good news.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Despite repeated warnings from the authorities, the company continued to operate in complete ________ of the environmental regulations.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. defiance. 💢 Explanation: - The word defiance means open resistance or refusal to obey authority. The fixed and commonly tested expression “in defiance of” perfectly fits the sentence. The phrase clearly conveys that the company knowingly ignored the environmental regulations despite receiving repeated warnings, which strongly indicates deliberate disobedience. Hence, this option is the most appropriate choice. 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG - • Option (1) - violation refers to the act of breaking a law or rule, and the phrase “in violation of” is grammatically acceptable. However, it does not strongly emphasize intentional resistance. The sentence context highlights wilful disregard despite warnings, which makes this option weaker than the correct answer. • Option (3) – ignorance means lack of knowledge or awareness. Using this word would imply that the company was unaware of the environmental regulations. This directly contradicts the phrase “despite repeated warnings,” which clearly shows that the company knew about the rules. Therefore, this option is logically incorrect. • Option (4) –contradiction refers to opposition between statements or ideas. The expression “in contradiction of regulations” is neither idiomatic nor commonly used in standard English. It fails to convey the idea of disobedience or resistance and does not fit the sentence grammatically.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Habitually speaking the truth/precisely accurate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Veracious. 👉 Let’s see the meaning of all words, • Mimicry - अनुकरण - The act of imitating or copying someone or something - The comedian's mimicry of the politician was spot on. • Payee - प्राप्तकर्ता - A person who receives payment - The payee's name was listed on the check. • Veracious - सत्यवादी - Habitually speaking the truth or precisely accurate - The journalist was known for being veracious in her reporting. • Mirthful - आनंदित - Full of joy or amusement - The mirthful atmosphere at the party was infectious.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To clear the decks
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. To remove obstructions . 💢 Explanation: The idiom “to clear the decks” means to remove obstacles, distractions, or hindrances so that one can prepare for action or focus fully on an important task. It is commonly used when preparations are being made before undertaking something serious or significant. 📌 Origin of the Idiom: This idiom originates from naval warfare. Before a battle, sailors cleared the decks of unnecessary items so that the crew could move freely and fight effectively. Over time, it came to mean removing all obstacles before starting an important activity. Example: Before launching the new project, the company decided to clear the decks by resolving all pending issues. Hindi: “to clear the decks” = रुकावटें हटाना / पूरी तैयारी करना / सभी बाधाओं को दूर करना

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Art consisting of a design made of small pieces of coloured stone/glass
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Mosaic. 👉 Let see the meaning of all words - • Gaggle - पक्षियों का झुंड - A gaggle of geese flew overhead. • Mosaic- मोज़ेक - Art consisting of a design made of small pieces of coloured stone/glass - The temple's floor was decorated with intricate mosaics. • Ventral - उदर से संबंधित - Relating to the abdomen or belly - The ventral side of the fish was pale. • Aviary - पक्षीशाला - A place where birds are kept - The zoo's aviary was home to many exotic bird species.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. As a result, many employees now prefer flexible work schedules over traditional office hours. B. This shift has increased productivity and improved work-life balance. C. The widespread adoption of remote work has transformed modern workplaces. D. Digital communication tools have made collaboration easier across different locations.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. CDBA. Sol:- The correct order is: C–D–B–A 💢 Explanation: • C is the best opening sentence because it introduces the main cause — the transformation in financial behaviour due to the growth of smartphones. • D logically follows, explaining how this transformation takes place through online wallets and UPI platforms. • B comes next because “This shift” clearly refers to the digital payment systems mentioned in sentence D. • A is the concluding sentence as “As a result” summarizes the final outcome of all the previous statements. ✅ Final paragraph: The rapid growth of smartphone usage has transformed the way people manage finances. Online wallets and UPI platforms have become an essential part of modern banking. This shift has significantly reduced the reliance on physical cash. As a result, many individuals now depend heavily on digital payments for everyday transactions.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Before they invented the electricity, people had to light homes by lamps.
options: Before electricity invented, homes were lit by lamps by people.
Before electricity was invented, people had to light homes by lamps.
Before electricity was invented by them, homes had to be lit.
Before electricity was invented, homes had to be lit by lamps.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Before electricity was invented, homes had to be lit by lamps.. Sol: In such sentences: • Active Structure: Sub + had to + V1 + Obj → People had to light homes by lamps. • Passive Structure: Obj + had to be + V3 (past participle) + by (agent if needed) → Homes had to be lit by lamps. We also transform the clause "they invented electricity" to its passive form: → electricity was invented. Putting it all together: Before electricity was invented, homes had to be lit by lamps.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect/direct speech. He said, "How wonderful it is to finally see the mountains after such a long journey!"
options: He said that it was wonderful to finally see the mountains after such a long journey.
He exclaimed with joy that it was wonderful to finally see the mountains after such a long journey.
He exclaimed how it was wonderful to finally see the mountains after a long journey.
He told that it was very wonderful finally seeing the mountains after the journey.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. He exclaimed with joy that it was wonderful to finally see the mountains after such a long journey.​​​​​ Sol: 💢 Change of Tense: • The reporting verb "said" becomes "exclaimed with joy" to reflect the emotional tone of the exclamatory sentence. • The present tense verb "is" changes to "was" because the reporting verb is in the past tense. • No change is needed for the infinitive phrase "to finally see the mountains" as it stays in its original form. • "Such a long journey" remains unchanged as it's part of the descriptive phrase. That: • Since it's an exclamatory sentence, it is reported using "that" — not "how", and the tone is turned into a statement [not a question or exclamation in form]. 👉 Correct structure: He exclaimed with joy that it was wonderful to finally see the mountains after such a long journey.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. do. 💢 Explanation - The subject “employees” is plural, so the correct auxiliary verb for forming the negative is do. The structure do not communicate is grammatically correct in the present tense. 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: • Does is used with singular subjects only. • Did shifts the sentence to the past tense, which is not intended. • Have cannot function as an auxiliary for forming negatives with base verbs in this structure.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. become. 💢 Explanation - This sentence states a general condition or habitual truth, which is commonly tested in exams. After conjunctions like “if” used for general conditions, the simple present tense is preferred. The structure “can struggle if they become overly dependent” correctly expresses a recurring possibility applicable to professionals in general. Hence, this option is grammatically and contextually correct. ✔️ 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG • Option (2) – became - This option uses the simple past tense, which would suggest a specific past situation. However, the sentence discusses a general professional tendency, not a completed past event. Using “became” breaks tense consistency with “can struggle” and is therefore incorrect. • Option (3) – are becoming - The present continuous tense implies an ongoing, temporary process happening at the moment. The sentence, however, talks about a general professional weakness, not a developing action in progress. Hence, this tense does not suit the context. • Option (4) – have become - The present perfect tense indicates a completed action with present relevance. While grammatically possible in isolation, it does not fit well with a general conditional statement introduced by “if” and clashes with the universal nature of the observation being made.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. erratic. 💢 Explanation - Erratic means inconsistent or unpredictable. The passage describes poor professional habits leading to weak leadership, which naturally results in irregular and unreliable decision-making. 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: • Judicious implies sound judgment. • Decisive suggests confidence and clarity. • Methodical indicates systematic behaviour, opposite to the idea conveyed.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. however. 💢 Explanation - The sentence contrasts technical expertise with accountability. However correctly signals opposition between competence and effectiveness. 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: • Therefore indicates consequence, not contrast. • Moreover adds information, rather than opposing it. • Meanwhile relates to time, not logic.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 –
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. out of. 💢 Explanation - The fixed expression break out of means to escape from a limiting condition. The sentence urges employees to escape complacency, making out of the correct choice. 👉 WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: • Break into means enter forcibly. • Break from suggests separation, not escape. • Break against is not idiomatically correct here.

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no correction is needed, select ‘No correction required’. The manager approved their all proposals without any objections.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. all their proposals. Sol: Replace “their all proposals” with “all their proposals.” 💢 Explanation: According to standard grammatical rules, quantifiers like “all” should come before possessive pronouns when they are used to modify a noun. 👉 The correct sentence should be: “The manager approved all their proposals without any objections.”

questions.question: Tuberculosis primarily affects which organ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Lungs. Tuberculosis (TB) is a contagious disease caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis. It mainly affects the lungs, where it causes symptoms like persistent cough, chest pain, and coughing up blood.

questions.question: With reference to the Partition of Bengal (1905), which of the following statements is/are correct? • The partition was opposed by the Indian National Congress and led to the Swadeshi Movement. • The partition was reversed in 1911 due to widespread protests and opposition from the Indian people. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Both 1 & 2 . • Opposition and Swadeshi Movement: The Indian National Congress (INC), led by leaders like Surendranath Banerjee and Krishna Kumar Mitra, spearheaded the opposition. This culminated in the Swadeshi Movement, which promoted the use of Indian goods and the boycott of British products. • Reversal in 1911: Facing relentless political unrest and the growing influence of revolutionary nationalism, the British government annulled the partition in 1911. The announcement was made by King George V during the Delhi Durbar, where the capital was also shifted from Calcutta to Delhi.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Pandit Lakhmi Chand Kalakar Samajik Samman Yojana in May 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Haryana. The Haryana Cabinet led by Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini launched the Pandit Lakhmi Chand Kalakar Samajik Samman Yojana on 5th May 2025. This scheme offers monthly financial help to elderly and economically weaker artistes in Haryana. It aims to support senior artists who have made significant contributions in fields like music, dance, painting, and theatre. The scheme honours veteran cultural practitioners who face financial hardship in their old age. It reflects the state’s commitment to preserving Haryana’s rich cultural heritage. It also promotes traditional arts by valuing and supporting the artists who have kept them alive.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: i. This gland has both endocrine and exocrine functions. ii. It secretes insulin and glucagon into the bloodstream. iii. Its digestive secretions are rich in bicarbonates and enzymes. These functions are performed by:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Pancreas. The pancreas is a heterocrine gland, performing both endocrine (hormones like insulin, glucagon) and exocrine (digestive enzymes) functions. Its secretion contains bicarbonates and enzymes, making it the complete digestive juice for all food types.

questions.question: Under which Act do the Environment Protection (Management of Contaminated Sites) Rules, 2025 fall?
options: Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Environment Protection Act, 1986. The Environment Protection (Management of Contaminated Sites) Rules, 2025 are notified under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, which is an umbrella legislation empowering the Central Government to frame rules for environmental protection, including hazardous substances and contaminated land.

questions.question: Consider the following: 1. Currency with the public 2. Demand deposits with banks 3. Time deposits with banks Which of these are included in Broad Money (M3) in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 1, 2 and 3. RBI publishes figures for four alternative measures of money supply, viz, M0, M1, M2 and M3. M3 is known as Broad Money. M3 includes : M3 = Currency with the public + Demand deposits with banks + Other deposits with RBI + Net time deposits of commercial banks or M3 = M1 + Net time deposits of Commercial banks

questions.question: When treaty of Purandhar was singed between the Rajput ruler Jai Singh I, who was commander of the Mughal Empire, and Maratha Shivaji?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1665. The Treaty of Purandar was signed in 1665 between the Rajput commander Jai Singh I, representing the Mughal Empire, and the Maratha leader Shivaji Maharaj.

questions.question: Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer from the code given below:- List-I List-II A Formation of new states 1. Part-2 of the Constitution states of India B Citizenship 2. Art.3 of the Constitution of India C Fundamental Rights 3. Art.323-A of the Constitution of India D Setting of Administrative Tribunals 4. Part-3 of the Constitution of India
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3. • Formation of New States: Article 3 a. Empowers the Indian Parliament to create new states by separating territory from an existing state or uniting multiple territories. b. Allows for the alteration of state boundaries, areas, and official names through ordinary legislation passed by a simple majority. c. Requires a prior recommendation from the President before a bill is introduced in Parliament. • Citizenship: Part II (Articles 5–11) a. Defines who was considered an Indian citizen at the commencement of the Constitution on January 26, 1950. b. Upholds the principle of "Single Citizenship," meaning there is no separate state-level citizenship in India. c. Article 11 specifically grants Parliament the exclusive power to regulate the acquisition and termination of citizenship by law. • Fundamental Rights: Part III (Articles 12–35) a. Guarantees essential civil liberties, including the Right to Equality, Freedom of Speech, and Protection of Life and Liberty. b. Referred to as the "Magna Carta of India," these rights are justiciable, meaning they are enforceable by courts. c. Citizens can directly approach the Supreme Court under Article 32 for the enforcement of these rights. • Administrative Tribunals: Article 323-A a. Inserted by the 42nd Amendment Act in 1976 to provide a specialized forum for resolving disputes related to public service conditions. b. Allows Parliament to establish tribunals like the Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) to handle grievances of government employees. c. Designed to reduce the burden on traditional courts and ensure faster, more expert adjudication of service-related matters.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the ‘Red Planet’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Mars. Mars is known as the ‘Red Planet’ due to its reddish appearance, which is caused by iron oxide [rust] on its surface.

questions.question: Where was the G7 Summit 2025 held?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Alberta. The 51st G7 Summit was held from 15 to 17 June 2025 in Kananaskis, Alberta, Canada. Key details of the event include:- • Major Themes: The summit focused on protecting communities, building energy security, accelerating the digital transition, and securing future partnerships. • Outcome Documents: Two significant documents were adopted: the Kananaskis Wildfire Charter and the G7 Critical Minerals Action Plan. • Outreach Participation: Several world leaders attended as outreach partners, including Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who addressed sessions on energy security and technology.

questions.question: Consider the following statements related to Dances:- I. Fazilka Heritage festival is celebrated in the state of Punjab. II. Valar Dance is perfomed by the people of Baiga tribe III. Mopin is an agricultural festival of Arunachal Pradesh celebrated by the Galo tribe. Which of the following statements is/are true?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Both I and III. Fazilka is a town in Punjab, and the Fazilka Heritage Festival is indeed celebrated there to promote local culture, traditions, and heritage. The Valar dance is not associated with the Baiga tribe. The Baiga tribe, primarily from Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh, is known for other traditional dances like Paika, Pardhauni and Saila. Mopin is a major agricultural festival celebrated by the Galo tribe in Arunachal Pradesh, typically to ensure a good harvest and ward off evil spirits. So, II statement is not correct.

questions.question: Law of inertia is also called?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Newtons’s First Law of Motion. Newton's first law states that an object remains in its position (in a state of rest or motion) unless an external force is applied on it. This is called the Law of Inertia.

questions.question: Who completely destructed pindari clan in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Lord Hastings. The Pindari clan in India was largely destroyed through military campaigns led by the British East India Company, specifically under the command of Governor-General Lord Hastings, during the Pindari War of 1817-1818.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: i. The Concurrent List in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from Australia. ii. The concept of single citizenship is taken from Canada. iii. The idea of the Preamble is borrowed from the U.S. Constitution. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. i and iii only. Single Citizenship (Statement ii): This statement is incorrect. While Canada does have a form of single citizenship, the Indian Constitution specifically borrowed the concept of single citizenship from the British Constitution (United Kingdom). In contrast, federal systems like the United States typically employ dual citizenship.

questions.question: Match the 2025 domestic tournament to its winning team: Column A Column B 1. Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy A. Vidharba 2. Ranji Trophy B. Haryana 3. Pro Kabaddi League (Season 11) C. Jharkhand 4. Durand Cup D. NorthEast
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1-C, 2-A, 3-B, 4-D. Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy: Jharkhand won their maiden title in the 2025-26 season final against Haryana. Ranji Trophy: Vidarbha won the 2024-25 season by defeating Kerala in the final. Pro Kabaddi League (Season 11): Haryana Steelers won their first PKL title by defeating Patna Pirates in the final, which concluded in December 2024. Durand Cup: NorthEast United won the 134th edition of the tournament by defeating Diamond Harbour FC in the final, which took place in August 2025.

questions.question: What was the literacy rate in India as per the 2011 Census?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 74.04%. Total Literacy: 74.04% Male Literacy: 82.14% Female Literacy: 65.46% Key Highlights from the 2011 Census:- • Highest Literacy Rate: Kerala recorded the highest literacy rate at 94.00%. • Lowest Literacy Rate: Bihar recorded the lowest literacy rate at 61.80%. • Growth: There was a significant improvement from the 2001 Census, where the overall literacy rate stood at 64.83%. • Urban vs. Rural: Literacy in urban areas was significantly higher at 87.7% compared to 73.5% in rural areas.

questions.question: The atomic number of an element represents the number of?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Protons. The atomic number of an element represents the total number of protons found in the nucleus of an atom. It is the fundamental property that defines an element's identity; for example, any atom with 6 protons is always carbon. While the number of electrons equals the number of protons in a neutral atom, the atomic number is strictly defined by protons because electron counts change when atoms become ions. Neutrons, meanwhile, determine the isotope, not the atomic number.

questions.question: Which committee recommended the introduction of Local Self-Government in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. B.R. Mehta Committee. B.R Mehta Committee recommended local self-government and allocation of more financial resources to village Panchayats.

questions.question: Which of the following does not give a positive test with Tollen’s reagent?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Acetone. Tollen's reagent (ammoniacal silver nitrate) is a mild oxidizing agent used to distinguish between aldehydes and ketones. Acetone does NOT give a positive test with Tollens’ reagent. (1) Formaldehyde: This is an aliphatic aldehyde. It reacts positively, forming a silver mirror. (2) Acetaldehyde: This is also an aliphatic aldehyde and gives a positive Tollen's test. (3) Benzaldehyde: Even though it is an aromatic aldehyde, it still reacts with Tollen's reagent to give a positive result (unlike Fehling's solution, which aromatic aldehydes do not react with).

questions.question: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has how many member states?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 57. Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has 57 member states. It is the second-largest intergovernmental organization after the UN, representing countries from the Middle East, Africa, Asia, and parts of Europe and South America.

questions.question: Article 124 of the Indian Constitution deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Establishment of Supreme Court. Article 124 of the Indian Constitution deals with: Establishment and Constitution of the Supreme Court of India It lays down: The appointment of Supreme Court judges, Qualifications for appointment Tenure and removal (including provisions for impeachment) Structure of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Peninsular Plateau and its associated features: i. The Deccan Trap basaltic formations originated during the Mesozoic era due to volcanic activity linked with the breakup of Gondwana. ii. The Vindhyan range forms the northern boundary of the Deccan Plateau and is older than the Aravalli Hills. iii. The Eastern Ghats were formed during the Paleozoic era and have remained relatively unaffected by subsequent tectonic activity. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. i and iii only. The Deccan Traps formed in the Mesozoic era due to volcanic activity linked to Gondwana's breakup. The Vindhyan range marks the northern boundary of the Deccan Plateau but is younger than the Aravalli Hills. The Eastern Ghats originated during the Paleozoic era and have been tectonically stable since. Thus, only statements i and iii are correct.

questions.question: Consider the following animals: 1. Hedgehog 2. Marmot 3. Pangolin To reduce the chance of being captured by predators, which of the above organisms rolls up/roll up and protects/protect its/their vulnerable parts?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 1 and 3. Hedgehogs are spiny mammals of the subfamily Erinaceinae, in the eulipotyphlan family Erinaceidae. All species of hedgehogs can roll into a tight ball in self-defense, causing all of the spines to point outwards. Marmots are large herbivores ground squirrels in the genus Marmota. They do not roll up when threatened. Pangolins, sometimes known as scaly anteaters are mammals of the order Pholidota. It can curl up into a ball when threatened, with its overlapping scales acting as armor, while it protects its face by tucking it under its tail. The scales are sharp, providing extra defense from predators.

questions.question: According to Bernoulli’s principle, when fluid velocity increases, pressure:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Decreases. Bernoulli's principle states that in a flowing fluid, an increase in velocity corresponds to a decrease in pressure. This is because the fluid's energy is conserved, so when kinetic energy (velocity) increases, the potential energy (pressure) must decrease to maintain the total energy.

questions.question: Which archaeological site is situated on the banks of Pravara River?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Daimabad. Daimabad is the Chalcolithic archaeological site located on the banks of the Pravara River in Ahmednagar district, Maharashtra. It is known for significant finds from the Jorwe culture and Late Harappan period, including bronze artifacts, especially the famous "Daimabad hoard".

questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. EI-HK ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Kavita_Test_30.01.2025_D1.png)

questions.question: Which of the following terms will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? DP4, GT9, JX25, MB49, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. PF121 1st term - (+3) 2nd term - (+4) 3rd term - square of prime no. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Sumit_S2.png)

questions.question: Your colleague at work has been caught taking bribes. He requests you not to tell anyone, promising you a share if you help cover it up. What should you do?
options: Accept the offer and keep silent.
Refuse the offer but do not report him.
Report the incident to the authorities immediately.
Ignore the situation as it does not involve you.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Report the incident to the authorities immediately. Accepting or ignoring corrupt behaviour is unethical and can have legal consequences. It is your responsibility to report such incidents to maintain integrity at the workplace. Ignoring it or accepting any offer makes you complicit.

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘COLD’ is written as ‘BDNPKMCE’ and ‘WIND’ is written as ‘VXHJMOCE’. How will ‘BIRD’ be written in that language?

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as shown in the given two matrices. The columns and rows of Matrix-I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix-II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices can by represented first by its column and next by its row, for example, ‘C’ can be represented by 43, 95, etc, and ‘K’ can be represented by 77, 00, etc. Similarly, identify the set for the word “HONEY”. Matrix - I - 0 1 2 3 4 0 K U N P F 1 T O E J H 2 D I G Q W 3 A T R E C 4 H Z B F Y Matrix - II - 5 6 7 8 9 5 G T I D C 6 N C X H Z 7 W O K R M 8 V I Y Z F 9 J Q S L B
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 86, 11, 56, 21, 78 The given code follows the logic as under: First digit of the number is taken from the column and subsequently, the second digit is taken from the row. In this manner, the correct code for HONEY is 86, 11, 56, 21, 78.

questions.question: In a certain code language, A + B means ‘A is the son of B’, A – B means ‘A is the sister of B’, A × B means ‘A is the wife of B’ and A ÷ B means ‘A is the father of B’. How is Q related to D if ‘Q ÷ E – R × C + D’?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Son's wife's father ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Sumit_S5.png) Son's Wife's Father

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: Some Mobile is Cover. Some cover is Pendrive. Conclusions: (I): No Mobile is Pendrive. (II): Some Mobile is Pendrive.
options: Either Conclusions I or II follow
Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Either Conclusions I or II follow ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Kavita_Test_30.01.2025_D2.png) Con 1. (Fig i) No mobile is pendrive Con 2. (Fig ii) Some mobile is pendrive Hence, Either I or II follows.

questions.question: If A means ÷, B means +, C means ×, D means -, What is the value of: 225 B 15 C 7 A 105 D 21=?

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 48 Let R = 31, L = 18, T = Total Formula – T = L + R – 1 T = 31 + 18 – 1 = 48

M is not a neighbour of L and is facing outwards. The person who sits opposite to M faces opposite direction of M and sits between F and J. A sits opposite to J and faces opposite direction of M. What is the position of L with respect to S (when counted from clockwise direction)?

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following set. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits.) (10 16 84) (9 8 73)
Which two signs and two numbers should be interchanged to make the given equation correct? 64 - 4 × 2 ÷ 8 + 7 = 19
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 4 and 8, ÷ and - Given equation is: 64 - 4 × 2 ÷ 8 + 7 = 19 Interchange 4 and 8, ÷ and - (64 ÷ 8) × 2 - 4 + 7 = 19 (8 × 2) - 4 + 7 = 19 16 - 4 + 7 = 19 12 + 7 = 19 19 = 19

questions.question: What will be the decimal equivalent for binary number 1011?
64 is related to 120 following a certain logic. Following the same logic, 81 is related to 135. To which of the following is related to 49, following the same logic?

The following are the criteria set by a Retail outlet for the selection of candidates for the post of Store In charge in a reputed mall. 1. The candidate must be a graduate in any stream with minimum of 50% marks. 2. The candidate must have Minimum of 5 years of Sales experience in Retail. 3. The age of the candidate should not be less than 25 years and more than 34 years as on 1st October, 2019. (a) If a candidate satisfies all the criteria except 2 as given above but has an MBA degree and has minimum of 2 years of work experience in sales, she/he must be sent to the General Manager, Recruitment for further interview. (b) If a candidate satisfies all the criteria except 3, but has more than 5 years experience as a Group Manager and above in Sales managing a team of sales executives, then she/he must be sent to the Regional Manager, Sales & Marketing, for further interview. Based on the above criteria and conditions, analyse and decide which of the following course of action should be taken for the candidate whose description is provided below. Please note that you are not to assume anything other than the given information. Sujoy is a graduate with 55% marks and MBA through Correspondence. He was born on Sept 20, 1990. He has 1 year of work experience as a sales executive and 2 years of experience as a Group Manager, Sales. On the basis of the information as given above, which of the following decisions would be appropriate
options: The candidate is to be selected.
The case is to be referred to the General Manager, Recruitment for further interview.
The candidate is not to be selected.
The case is to be referred to the Regional Manager, Sales & Marketing, for further interview.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. The case is to be referred to the General Manager, Recruitment for further interview. According to the given information. 1) Graduation criterion is satisfied. 2) This condition is not satisfiedbut condition a) is satisfied, so the case is to be referred to the General Manager, Recruitment. 3) Age criterion is satisfied. Hence, option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following calendars will be the same as the calendar for the year 2009?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 2015 Concepts 1) A leap year calendar repeats in 28 years. 2) Calendar of ordinary year in 6 or 11 years. 3) Calendar of ordinary year just after a leap year in 6 years. (eg. 2009) 4) For the next year after leap year in 11 years. (eg. 2010, 2011)

questions.question: By selling an article for ₹1520, Anu suffers as much loss as she would have gained by selling it at 5% profit. If she sells it for ₹ 1960, then her profit percentage is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 22.5%. Sol: Let CP=100 unit SP at 5% profit would be = 105 units SP at 5% loss would be = 95 units According to question, 95 =1520Rs \(CP(100\mathrm{\ units\ })=\frac{1520}{95}\times100=1600\) \(\mathrm{\% profit\ }=\frac{1960-1600}{1600}\) =22.5%

questions.question: The angle of depression of a point situated at a distance of 60 m from the base of a tower is 60°. The height of the tower is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. \(60\sqrt3\) m. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/1.png) Tan60° = AB/BC AB = x = \(60\sqrt3\) m

questions.question: If the variance of 6 values is 13.69, then what is its standard deviation?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 3.7. Standard deviation = \(\sqrt{Variance} \)= \(\sqrt{13.69}\) = 3.7

questions.question: Find the fourth proportion of 36, 102, and 30.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 85. ATQ, 36 : 102 :: 30 : x ⇒\( \frac{36}{102} = \frac{30}{x}\) ⇒ \( x = 85\)

questions.question: Two candidates P and Q contested in an election. 75% of the registered voters are P supporters. If 64% of the P supporters and 40% of the Q supporters are expected to vote for candidate P, then what percentage of the registered voters are expected to vote for candidate P?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 58%. Let total votes = 100 P supporter = 75 Q supporter = 25 Expected to vote for P = 64% of P’s supporter + 40% of Q’s supporter = 48 + 10 = 58 = \(\frac{58}{100}×100=58%\)

questions.question: The base of a right pyramid is an equilateral triangle of side 10√3 cm. If the total surface area of the pyramid is 270√3 cm2, its height is.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 12 cm. Sol: Total surface area of pyramid = area of base + area of 3 lateral surface ⇒ \(270\sqrt3 = \frac{\sqrt3}{4}[10\sqrt3]2 + 3 [\ \frac{1}{2} × 10\sqrt3\) × lateral height of triangle] Lateral height of the pyramid = 13 ∴ Height of pyramid = \(\sqrt{{(13)}^2-{(5)}^2}\) = 12 cm

questions.question: In a ∆ABC, ‘D’ is the mid-point of BC and \angleC = 50°. A is joined to D and AD = BD. The measure of \angleBAC is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 90°. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/2.png) As ‘D’ is the mid-point of BC; i.e. BD = CD and given AD = BD. Thus, AD = CD \(\Rightarrow\) \(\angle DAC\) = \(\angle DCA\) = 50° So, \angleADC = 80° And, since AD = BD \(\Rightarrow\) \(\angle DAB\) = \(\angle DBA\) = 40° Hence, \(\angle BAC\) = 90°

questions.question: If \(\frac{\sec{\theta}+\tan{\theta}}{\sec{\theta}-\tan{\theta}}\) = \(\frac{5}{3}\) then, find the value of sinθ.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(\frac{1}{4}\). Given that \(\frac{\sec{\theta}+\tan{\theta}}{\sec{\theta}-\tan{\theta}}=\frac{5}{3}\) = \(\frac{\frac{1}{\cos{\theta}}\ +\ \frac{\sin{\theta}}{\cos{\theta}}}{\frac{1}{\cos{\theta}}\ -\ \frac{\sin{\theta}}{\cos{\theta}}}=\frac{5}{3}\) = \(\frac{1\ +\ \sin{\theta}}{1-\ \sin{\theta}}=\frac{5}{3}\) = \(\frac{1}{\sin{\theta}}=\frac{8}{2}=4\) = \(\sin{\theta}=\frac{1}{4}\)

questions.question: A solid metal cone of 15 cm height and 7 cm radius is melted and recast into two cylinders in the proportion of 1:2 [volume], keeping the height 15 cm. What would be the percentage change in the flat surface area before and after [use r=\(\frac{22}{7}\)]?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. \(33\frac{1}{3}%\). ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/3.png) \(\mathrm{volume\ }=3{:\ }1{\ }:2\) \(\frac{R^2}{3}:{r_1}^2:{r_2}^2=3:1:2\) \(\therefore R^2:{r_1}^2:{r_2}^2=9:1:2{\ }(R=\mathrm{\ radius\ of\ cone}\)) ratio of flat surface Area → cone : cylinder 1 : cylinder 2 9 : 2 : 4 Since r₂ is the radius of the second cylinder \(\mathrm{change\ in\ frat\ surface\ area\ }=\frac{9-6}{9}\times100\) \( =33\frac{1}{3}\)%

questions.question: 15 days are required to complete a work by A and B, 20 days required by B and C and 12 days required by C and A. In how many days C alone can complete the work?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 30 days. ![](https://thepunditstesting.hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/4.png) Time Taken by A,B,C = \(\frac{60}{\frac{(4+3+5)}{2}}\) =10 ⇒ Efficiency of A + B + C=6 Efficiency of A + B=4 Efficiency of C=2 Hence, time taken by C = \(\frac{60}{2}\) = 30 days

questions.question: A bag contains 9 white balls and x black balls, the odd against drawing a black ball are 3:7. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 21. Odd against drawing a black ball means the black ball has not been drawn; i.e. \(\frac{9}{x}\ = \frac{3}{7} \Rightarrow 𝑥 = 21 \)

questions.question: What is the value of the expression: \(\left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2}}+7^2\left(\ \sqrt[3]{\frac{1}{343}}\right)\times\left(125\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}+\left(216\right)^\frac{1}{3}\left(27\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}\div\frac{\sqrt9}{\sqrt[4]{256}}-5^0\)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 106/15. \(\left({16}^{-1}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2}}+49\ \left(\sqrt[3]{\frac{1}{7^3}}\right)\times\left(5^3\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}+\left(6^3\right)^\frac{1}{3}\left(3^3\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}\div\frac{3}{4}-1\) \(\sqrt{16}+\frac{49}{7}\times\left(\frac{1}{5}\right)+6\times\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)\div\frac{3}{4}-1\) \(=4+\frac{7}{5}+\frac{6}{3}\times\frac{4}{3}-1\) \(=4+\frac{7}{5}+\frac{8}{3}-1\) \(=\frac{27}{5}+\frac{8}{3}-1\) \(=\frac{121-15}{15}\) = \(\frac{106}{15}\)

questions.question: The base of right prism is trapezium whose parallel sides are 11 cm and 15 cm and the distance between them is 9 cm. If the volume of the prism is 1778.40 cm, then the height [in cm] of the prism will be:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 15.2. Volume of prism = Base area × height 1778.40 cm3 = \(\frac{1}{2}\) × [11+15] × 9 × height 15.2 cm = height

questions.question: Three runners A, B, and C start together from the same point on a circular track of length 360 meters and run in the same direction. Their speeds are 6 m/s, 8 m/s, and 9 m/s respectively. After how many seconds will all three runners meet again at the starting point simultaneously?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 360 seconds. Time for each runner to complete one full round: A = \(\frac{360}{6}=60\ sec\) B = \(\frac{360}{8}=45\ sec\) C = \(\frac{360}{9}=40\ sec\) LCM of 60, 45, and 40 = 360 They will all meet again after 360 seconds

questions.question: The volume of a cuboid is 8100 cubic cm. The areas of two adjacent faces are 450 square cm and 540 square cm. What is the area of the other adjacent face?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 270 cm2. Given, volume of the cuboid= lbh = 8100 cm3 lbh × lbh = lb × lh × bh ⇒ 8100 × 8100 = 450 × 540 × bh ⇒ bh = 270 cm2 Alternate Method: Given, lbh = 8100 cubic cm and areas of two adjacent faces (suppose) (l × b) & (l × h) are given. And, lb = 450 cm2 ----(i) Thus, h = \(\frac{8100}{450}\) = 18 cm and when lh = 540 cm2 ⇒ l = 30 cm Now, from equation (i), b = \(\frac{450}{30}\) = 15 cm Hence, area of the other adjacent face = b h = 15 × 18 = 270 cm2

questions.question: In a row of 10 seats, how many ways can 4 people sit next to each other?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 168. To ensure the 4 people sit next to each other, we take them as a single block. The number of possible starting positions M for a block size N in a row of P seats is given by: M = N - P + 1 Here, M = 10 – 4 + 1 = 7 Because the 4 people are distinct individuals, they can arrange themselves within those 4 seats in different orders. The number of ways to arrange: 4! = 4×3×2×1 = 24 The total number of ways is the product of the number of possible block positions and the number of ways the people can be arranged within that block: 24×7 = 168 ways.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: • If \({sin}^2\ A=\frac{9}{25} then, {tan}^2\ A=\frac{9}{16}\), if A is acute. • The value of \({sec}^2x-{tan}^2x\) is always equal to 1, for all values of x for which these functions are defined. • If \(\tan{7\theta}.\tan{2\theta}=1\) then \(\tan{12\theta=\sqrt3}\). Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1 and 2 only. Statement 1: \({sin}^2\ A=\frac{9}{25}\), and A is acute, then \({cos}^2\ A=1-\frac{9}{25}=\frac{16}{25}\) So, \({\ tan}^2\ A=\frac{{sin}^2A}{{cos}^2A}=\ \frac{9}{16}\), Hence Statement 1 is true. Statement 2 : \({sec}^2x-{tan}^2x=1\) valid for all x where cos x≠0. Hence, Statement 2 is true. Statement 3: tan7θ.tan2θ = 1 Then, 7θ + 2θ = 90 i.e. 9θ = 90, θ = 10 So, tan12θ = tan12θ = - √3 Hence, statement 3 is False.

questions.question: The average weight of a group increases by 4.5 kg when a person weighing 72.5 kg is replaced by another person weighing 117.5 kg. Find the number of people in the group.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 10. Number of people = \(\frac{117.5-72.5}{4.5}=\frac{45}{4.5} =10\)

questions.question: If the mean of x + 7, 2x + 8, 2x – 3 and 3x – 4 is 22, then the value of x is?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 10. Mean = \(\frac{sum\ of\ numbers}{total\ numbers}\) 22 = \(\frac{x\ +\ 7+2x\ +\ 8+\ 2x-3+\ 3x-4}{4}\) = 8x + 8 = 88 = x = \(\frac{80}{8}\) = 10

questions.question: A, B and C invest in a partnership. A invest 5 times of B and B invest 3/5 times of C’s investment. If the total profit at the end of the year is ₹49,335 then, find the share of C in the profit. (in ₹)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 10,725. Ratio of Investment = Ratio of Profit Ratio of investment of A, B and C = 15 : 3 : 5 Share of C = \(\frac{5}{23}\times49,335\) = ₹10,725

questions.question: Find the sum of all positive multiples of 7 less than 100?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 735. Multiples of 7 less than 100 = 7, 14, 21, ……. 98 n = \(\frac{98-7}{7}+1=\frac{91}{7}+1=14\) Sum = \(\frac{n}{2}\left(a+l\right)=\frac{14}{2}\left(7+98\right)=7\times105=735\)

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus are 24 cm and 32 cm. A parallelogram has the twice the area as the rhombus, but its altitude is equal to half the side of the rhombus. Find the base of the parallelogram.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 76.8. Side of Rhombus = \(\sqrt{\left(\frac{d_1}{2}\right)^2++\left(\frac{d_2}{2}\right)^2}\) = \(\sqrt{{12}^2+{16}^2}=\ \sqrt{144+256}=\ \sqrt{400}\) = 20 cm Area of rhombus = \(\frac{1}{2}\ \times\ d_1\times d_2 = \frac{1}{2}\ \times\ 24\times 32\) = 384 cm2 Height of the parallelogram = \(\frac{20}{2}\) = 10 cm So, Area of parallelogram = \(384 \times2\) Base × 10 = 768 Base = 76.8 cm

questions.question: Rs. 3600 was given on interest in two parts. If simple interest on the first part in 3 years with 6% interest rate is equal to simple interest of second part in 6 years with 12% interest rate. What is the second part?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Rs. 720. Let, Rs. A were given on 6% interest rate for 3 years and Rs. B were given on 12% interest rate for 6 years. \(\frac{A\times6\times3}{100} = \frac{B\times12\times6}{100} \Rightarrow \frac{A}{B} = \frac{4}{1} = \frac{4x}{x}\) It means 5x = 3600 \(\Rightarrow\) x = Rs. 720 = Second Part

questions.question: If \({12x}^2-21x+1 = 0\), then find the value of \(16x^2+\frac{1}{{9x}^2}\).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. \(46\frac{1}{3}\). \({12x}^2-21x+1=0\) ⇒\(\frac{{12x}^2}{3x}-\frac{21x}{3x}+\frac{1}{3x}=0\) ⇒ \(4x-7+\frac{1}{3x}=0\) ⇒ \(4x+\frac{1}{3x}=7\) Squaring on both sides ⇒ \(16x^2+\frac{1}{{9x}^2}+2\ \times4x\times\frac{1}{3x}=49\) ⇒ \(16x^2+\frac{1}{{9x}^2}=49-\frac{8}{3}=\frac{139}{3}=46\frac{1}{3}\)

questions.question: After offering two successive discounts, a laptop with a marked price of ₹48,000 is sold for ₹36,288. If the value of the first discount is 20%, what is the percentage value of the second discount?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 5.5%. MP of Laptop = Rs. 48,000 Price after first discount of 20% = 48000 \(\times\frac{4}{5}=38400\) Price after second discount = 36,288 Discount % given = \(\frac{38400-36288}{38400}\times100 = \frac{2112}{384}=5.5\ \%\)
